_schema_note: Phase B flux model parameters. Each parameter cites primary source + verification tier. Uncertainty bounds drive sensitivity analysis in scripts/flux_model.py.

total_daily_urate_production_mg_per_day.source: engineered-yeast-uricase-proposal.md / cross-validation.md citing standard physiology references; consistent with Choi 2005 and most clinical-pharm reviews

renal_excretion_fraction_normal_kidney.source: Takada 2014 PMID 24940679 mouse KO confirms ~2/3 renal in WT; multiple historical tracer studies

intestinal_excretion_fraction_normal_kid.source: Takada 2014; PMC8907525 review; Sorensen 1962/1965 historical tracers

Km_urate_mM.source: Nakayama 2011 PMID 22132962 membrane vesicle assay (Km = 8.24 +/- 1.44 mM)

abcg2_transporter_kinetics._note_on_in_vivo_application: Vmax in vitro is per mg of ABCG2 protein, not per intestine. We do not have a reliable absolute total ABCG2 protein abundance per human jejunum + ileum + colon. Therefore the flux model uses RELATIVE flux scaling (functional class A x B x C, where C is the WT-baseline daily intestinal urate excretion of ~230 mg/day) rather than an absolute Vmax-based first-principles calculation. The Miyazaki 2025 measured ratios ARE the empirical anchor.

abcg2_q141k_relative_function._methodology: Relative function = (functional ABCG2 surface expression) x (per-protein transport rate). Q141K causes a ~63% reduction in surface expression PLUS some per-protein rate reduction (~53% at the transport-rate level). Combined, the heterozygous Q141K phenotype is ~75% functional and homozygous is ~50% functional. This is the Japanese functional-classification framework (Matsuo 2014 / Miyazaki 2025) we adopt.

miyazaki_2025_empirical_anchor._purpose: These are the direct in-vivo measurements that anchor the flux model. The flux model uses the RATIO of secretion rates between functional classes — robust to absolute scaling.
miyazaki_2025_empirical_anchor._note_on_median_vs_mean: The mean ratio is much larger than the median ratio because of skewed distributions and small n in the 50%-functional group (n=5). Prefer median for central estimate; report mean for sensitivity bound. The flux model's central estimate uses MEDIAN ratios (more conservative, less prone to outlier dominance).

luminal_urate_concentration.interpretation: Baseline jejunal urate is ~0.6 uM, FAR below ABCG2 Km of 8.24 mM = 8240 uM. The transporter operates in the LINEAR regime (rate proportional to substrate, not saturated). This means: (a) reducing lumen urate via uricase increases the gradient roughly proportionally; (b) the ~0.3 mM cited in the comp-019 brief is an upper-bound figure from inflamed/post-meal physiology, not steady-state baseline. We use 0.59 uM (Miyazaki) as central estimate and 50 uM as sensitivity upper bound (post-fructose / post-purine-meal spike).

Km_uricase_uM.source: Sanofi rasburicase regulatory label (the closest validated A. flavus uricase Km in clinical use); literature variable across in-house assay conditions

specific_activity_U_per_mg._note: 1 U = 1 umol urate degraded per minute at pH 8.5, 37C

uricase_kinetic_priors._note_on_lumen_pH: Colonic pH ~7-8, near uricase pH-optimum (8.5). Small intestinal pH 6-7, sub-optimal but still functional. Effective specific activity in vivo likely 50-80% of the pH-8.5 in-vitro value. We use 6 U/mg (~75% of the label value) for in vivo flux model central estimate.

uricase_dose_scenarios._methodology: Three uricase-dose scenarios for the engineered koji / S. boulardii product, in mg active enzyme reaching the colon per day. Anchored against ALLN-346 dosing (Phase 2a, 5 capsules tid) and S. boulardii engineered ACS Synth Bio 2025 yield (365 umol/h/OD).
uricase_dose_scenarios._note: ALLN-346 phase 2a equivalent is ~50 mg/day total enzyme; mid-dose 25 mg/day matches the engineered-yeast-uricase-proposal.md home-fermentation feasibility window.

renal_compensation_fraction._note: When intestinal urate excretion is reduced (due to Q141K) or augmented (due to gut-lumen uricase), the kidney partially compensates by adjusting tubular handling. Matsuo 2014 showed Q141K dysfunction shifts load to the kidney (UUE up); but in CKD/aging/typical gout patients the renal compensation reserve is constrained. We model 30% partial compensation as central estimate (intestinal flux delta is 30% offset by renal counter-adjustment).

serum_urate_typical_baseline_mg_dL._purpose: Reference point for converting absolute urate flux into delta-SUA
serum_urate_typical_baseline_mg_dL._note: Uric acid MW 168.11 -> 1 mg/dL = 10 mg/L = 59.48 umol/L = 59.48 uM

extracellular_urate_pool_size_mg._note: Total miscible urate pool in gout patients ~1200 mg (Choi 2005, standard physiology). For non-gout pool ~600-800 mg. This sets the buffering capacity for any single-day flux delta.
